sections.paragraph:
ἀκλινής, ές (κλίνω ‘lean’; Pla. et al.; Meleager [I b.c.]: Anth. Pal. 12, 158, 4 φιλία; Lucian, Encom. Demosth. 33 ἀ. τὴν ψυχήν; Aelian, VH 12, 64; POxy 904, 9; Philo, Mos. 1, 30, Virtut. 158, Spec. Leg. 2, 2 ὅρκος ἀ.; 4 Macc 6:7; 7:3; Ath. 31, 3; ὁ τὸν κανόνα τῆς ἀληθείας ἀκλινῆ ἐν ἑαυτῷ κατέχω Iren. 1, 9, 4 [Harv. I 88, 1]; ‘bending to neither side’) without wavering τὴν ὁμολογίαν ἀ. κατέχειν hold fast the confession without wavering Hb 10:23.—M-M. Spicq.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 36
